title: cornus sericea f. californica
intro: Cornus sericea f. californica, also known as Red Flowering Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to the western coast of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of Red Flowering Dogwood are commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
